summary: Phase 2 Add-Location Optimization backend regression: 15/15 tests PASS. New `data_quality_score` and `data_quality_signals` fields on SpotCreateIn accepted, clamp validator works (150→100, -5→0, 999→100 — verified in response AND persisted to Mongo), `client_quality` sub-doc is correctly written with {score, signals, captured_at}, and the two raw fields do NOT leak to top-level on the spot document. Backwards-compat verified: public/draft/private spot create still 200 without the new fields. Pre-existing validators still reject null-island (0,0) and out-of-range lat (>90) with 422. Pre-existing endpoints still behave: GET /api/spots/check-duplicates 200, DELETE /api/spots/{id} 200, GET /api/me/park-session 401 unauth / 200 authed, POST /api/uploads/image 200 multipart. No regressions detected from the `doc.pop(...)` changes in create_spot.
critical_code_review_comments: Phase 2 implementation in routes/spots.py looks correct: `client_quality` is only written when at least one of score/signals is provided; otherwise the field is omitted (good — avoids polluting older docs).
Pydantic `_clamp_quality` validator correctly handles None / non-int input and clamps to [0,100]. Verified via Mongo read that 999 lands as 100.

doc.pop('data_quality_score') and doc.pop('data_quality_signals') are correctly stripped before the merged dict is written, so the raw fields never leak to top-level. Confirmed via direct Mongo lookup.

captured_at is a python datetime (utcnow()) — fine for Mongo (BSON date). If admin tooling exposes it via JSON it should be serialized as ISO; the GET /api/spots/{id} endpoint does NOT expose client_quality so no serialization risk surfaced here.

Free-plan upload cap path is bypassed for admin (limits_for returns max_uploads >= 10_000 for the comp_elite super_admin used in tests). Recommend a follow-up test with a Free-plan user to assert the new fields do not interact with the paywall path.
No changes to lat/lng/notes/land_access validators — null-island and lat>90 still reject with 422 as expected.
seed_data_creation: 9 TEST_-prefixed spots created during the run; all cleaned up by TestZCleanup at the end (verified via DELETE 200 responses in backend logs).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 Add-Location Optimization backend is solid. The new optional fields persist correctly under `client_quality` on the spot doc and the clamp logic works in both directions. If the main agent ships Phase 3 of this work, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_phase2_add_location.py — it's idempotent and self-cleaning. Frontend Fast-Add UI was NOT tested in this iteration (explicitly out of scope per the request).
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs surfaced. Initial test run had 2 failures due to missing MONGO_URL/DB_NAME in the pytest process env; fixed by adding `dotenv.load_dotenv(backend/.env)` at the top of the test file. All 15 tests then passed.
